current_capabilities.container_management: Compose-only via service table (start/stop/restart/logs/exec per service)
current_capabilities.image_management: None (images only shown as labels on containers)
current_capabilities.resource_monitoring: container_stats() exists in client.py but not used in UI

category_1_container_management.title: Universal Container Management
category_1_container_management.description: View and manage ALL containers, not just compose project containers. Docker Desktop and OrbStack show all containers with full lifecycle control.
category_1_container_management.current_gap: dockym only discovers containers via compose file scanning. Standalone containers (docker run) are invisible.

inspiration.docker_desktop: Full container list with start/stop/restart/remove/logs/inspect/exec/attach actions. Terminal access per container.
inspiration.orbstack: Containers shown in menu bar, 2-way file sharing, volume file access, container domains.
inspiration.podman_desktop: List, search, inspect, connect, run, stop containers. Play Kubernetes YAML locally.

features.title: All Containers View
features.description: New tab or sidebar section showing ALL running and stopped containers (not just compose-managed). Include container ID, name, image, status, ports, created date.
features.implementation: Add a new view to MainWindow or a tabbed interface. Use docker.containers.list(all=True) from client.py (already exists). Show compose vs standalone distinction.

features.title: Container Lifecycle Actions
features.description: Full CRUD for any container: start, stop, restart, pause, unpause, remove, force remove. Add confirmation dialogs for destructive actions.
features.implementation: Extend client.py with container action methods. Add context menu or action panel for container view.

features.title: Container Inspect
features.description: Show full container inspect JSON with formatted view. Docker Desktop provides detailed container info including mount points, network config, environment variables, labels.
features.implementation: New InspectDialog showing JSON output from client.containers.get(c).attrs with syntax highlighting.

features.title: Container Terminal (Attach/Exec)
features.description: Interactive terminal access to any container. OrbStack and Docker Desktop both provide this. Currently dockym only has basic exec.
features.implementation: Enhance ExecDialog with interactive PTY mode, or add a terminal emulator widget (QTerminalWidget from PySide6).

features.title: Container Top (Process List)
features.description: Show running processes inside a container (docker top). Useful for debugging without exec.
features.implementation: New tab in container detail view using client.containers.get(c).top().

category_2_image_management.title: Image Management
category_2_image_management.description: Full image lifecycle management. Docker Desktop provides image list, pull, push, build, remove, tag, and history features.
category_2_image_management.current_gap: dockym has ZERO image management. Images are only shown as text labels in the service table.

inspiration.docker_desktop: Image list with size, tags, created date. Pull from registries, build from Dockerfile, remove unused images. Docker Scout security scanning.
inspiration.orbstack: Image file access, volume file access, build images quickly.
inspiration.podman_desktop: Pull/push images, manage registries, build from Containerfile/Dockerfile.
inspiration.rancher_desktop: Built-in container images, pull from registries.

features.title: Image List View
features.description: List all local Docker images with repository, tag, ID, size, created date. Sort by name, size, or date. Filter by search text.
features.implementation: New ImagesView widget using docker.images.list(). Add to tabbed interface.

features.title: Image Pull
features.description: Pull images from Docker Hub or custom registries. Show pull progress with layers. Docker Desktop has a search + pull UI.
features.implementation: PullDialog with registry input, image name, tag selection. Use docker.images.pull() with progress callback.

features.title: Image Remove
features.description: Remove images with dependency check. Show which containers are using an image before removal.
features.implementation: Confirm dialog showing dependent containers. Use docker.images.remove() with force option.

features.title: Image History
features.description: Show image layer history (docker image history). Useful for understanding image composition and size per layer.
features.implementation: New dialog showing layers from docker.images.get(img).history().

features.title: Image Build
features.description: Build images from Dockerfile with context path selection. Show build progress output. Podman Desktop and Docker Desktop both support this.
features.implementation: BuildDialog with Dockerfile path, build context, build args. Use docker.images.build() with stream output.

category_3_volume_management.title: Volume Management
category_3_volume_management.description: Manage Docker volumes: list, inspect, remove, create. OrbStack provides volume file access, Docker Desktop shows volumes in dashboard.
category_3_volume_management.current_gap: dockym only has prune functionality for volumes. No listing, inspection, or creation.

inspiration.docker_desktop: Volume list with size, mount point, created date. Volume details showing which containers use each volume.

features.title: Volume List View
features.description: List all Docker volumes with name, driver, mount point, created date. Show which containers are using each volume.
features.implementation: New VolumesView widget using docker.volumes.list(). Cross-reference with containers.

features.title: Volume Inspect
features.description: Show full volume inspect JSON with formatted view. Display labels, options, scope, and mount details.
features.implementation: Dialog using docker.volumes.get(name).attrs.

features.title: Volume Create
features.description: Create new volumes with name, driver, and labels. Docker Desktop allows creating named volumes from the UI.
features.implementation: Simple creation dialog with name input and driver selection.

features.title: Volume Remove
features.description: Remove unused volumes with confirmation. Show which containers are using the volume before removal.
features.implementation: Confirm dialog with dependency check. Use docker.volumes.get(name).remove(force=True).

category_4_network_management.title: Network Management
category_4_network_management.description: View and manage Docker networks. Show network topology, connected containers, and subnet configuration.
category_4_network_management.current_gap: dockym only prunes networks. No viewing, inspection, or creation.

inspiration.docker_desktop: Network visualization showing connected containers. Network details with subnet, gateway, IP range.
inspiration.orbstack: Domain names for containers, IPv6 support, DNS integration, VPN compatibility.

features.title: Network List View
features.description: List all Docker networks with name, driver, subnet, gateway, connected container count. Show bridge/host/none special networks.
features.implementation: New NetworksView widget using docker.networks.list().

features.title: Network Inspect
features.description: Show network inspect JSON with formatted view. Display IPAM config, containers connected, options.
features.implementation: Dialog using docker.networks.get(name).attrs.

features.title: Network Topology Visualization
features.description: Simple graph showing networks and connected containers. OrbStack and Docker Desktop show network relationships visually.
features.implementation: New widget using QGraphicsScene with nodes (containers) and edges (networks). Keep it simple - no need for complex graph libraries.

category_5_resource_monitoring.title: Resource Monitoring Dashboard
category_5_resource_monitoring.description: Real-time CPU, memory, disk, and network usage for containers and the Docker host. Docker Desktop shows resource usage graphs.
category_5_resource_monitoring.current_gap: container_stats() exists in client.py but is NOT used anywhere in the UI. The function returns raw stats data but nothing displays it.

inspiration.docker_desktop: CPU, memory, disk usage graphs per container. Resource limits configuration.
inspiration.orbstack: Low CPU usage display, memory on demand, dynamic memory.
inspiration.podman_desktop: View allocated memory, CPU and storage for Podman machines.

features.title: Container Stats Panel
features.description: Show real-time CPU%, memory usage, network I/O, and block I/O for selected container. Update every 2-5 seconds.
features.implementation: New StatsWidget using container_stats() from client.py. Parse CPU percentage and memory usage from the stats response. Add to ActionPanel or as a new tab.

features.title: System Info Dashboard
features.description: Show Docker system info: total containers, images, volumes, disk usage, Docker version, storage driver, kernel version. Docker Desktop shows this on the home screen.
features.implementation: New SystemInfoWidget using docker.info() and docker.disk_usage(). Display in header or a dedicated panel.

features.title: Disk Usage Overview
features.description: Show disk usage breakdown: images, containers, volumes, build cache. Docker Desktop shows this in the prune dialog area.
features.implementation: Enhance PruneDialog or create new DiskUsageWidget using client.disk_usage(). Show before/after prune.

category_6_quick_actions.title: Quick Actions & Batch Operations
category_6_quick_actions.description: Perform batch operations on multiple containers/projects at once. Docker Desktop allows starting/stopping all containers.
category_6_quick_actions.current_gap: dockym only operates on one service at a time. No batch operations.

inspiration.docker_desktop: Start/stop all containers. Remove all stopped containers.

features.title: Start/Stop All Project Services
features.description: Start or stop all services in a project at once. Right-click on project node to access these actions.
features.implementation: Add project-level context menu in ServiceTree. Use project_up/project_down from compose.py (already exists in DockerPool).

features.title: Prune Enhancements
features.description: Show disk usage before pruning. Allow selective pruning by label, dangling images only, etc. Show space reclaimed after prune.
features.implementation: Enhance PruneDialog with disk_usage() preview and post-prune summary.

features.title: Bulk Container Actions
features.description: Select multiple containers and perform actions: start all, stop all, remove stopped. Inspired by Docker Desktop's multi-select.
features.implementation: Add checkbox selection to ContainersView. Batch action toolbar.

category_7_search_and_filter.title: Global Search & Filter
category_7_search_and_filter.description: Search across all resources: containers, images, volumes, networks. Filter by status, project, image, etc.
category_7_search_and_filter.current_gap: dockym has a search bar but only filters services in the tree. No cross-resource search.

inspiration.docker_desktop: Search containers, images. Filter by status.

features.title: Enhanced Service Search
features.description: Improve existing search to also match container names, image names, ports, and project paths. Add status filter dropdown (running/stopped/all).
features.implementation: Extend ServiceTree.set_filter() to match additional fields. Add filter dropdown above search bar.

features.title: Quick Switcher (Ctrl+P)
features.description: Command palette style quick switcher to jump to any container, image, or project. Inspired by VS Code's Ctrl+P.
features.implementation: New QuickSwitcherDialog with fuzzy search. Show results as you type.

category_8_notifications.title: Enhanced Notifications
category_8_notifications.description: Better notification system for container state changes. Docker Desktop and OrbStack both provide rich notifications.
category_8_notifications.current_gap: dockym has basic tray notifications on operation completion. Docker events trigger status bar updates but no rich notifications.

inspiration.docker_desktop: Notifications for container crashes, image pull completion, build completion.
inspiration.orbstack: Containers in menu bar, notifications from Linux.
inspiration.podman_desktop: Quickly check activity status from tray.

features.title: Container State Change Notifications
features.description: Show tray notification when a container changes state (start, stop, die, OOM). Already have DockerEventsWorker but only updates status bar.
features.implementation: Extend _on_docker_event() in MainWindow to show tray notification with container name and action.

features.title: Notification Preferences
features.description: Allow user to configure which events trigger notifications. Disable notifications for specific event types.
features.implementation: Add notification settings to Config and SettingsDialog.

features.title: Tray Menu Enhancements
features.description: Show quick-access actions in tray context menu: show running containers count, start/stop all, quick prune. OrbStack shows containers in menu bar.
features.implementation: Enhance TrayManager._setup_menu() with dynamic items showing container counts and quick actions.

ui_architecture_proposal.current: Single tab with service tree + action panel. Projects shown in tree, services as children.

tabs.name: Projects
tabs.description: Current service tree view (compose projects). Keep existing functionality.

tabs.name: Containers
tabs.description: All Docker containers view. List with full lifecycle actions.

tabs.name: Images
tabs.description: Docker images list. Pull, build, remove, inspect.

tabs.name: Volumes
tabs.description: Docker volumes list. Create, inspect, remove.

tabs.name: Networks
tabs.description: Docker networks list. Inspect, topology view.

implementation_notes: Use QTabWidget in MainWindow._setup_ui() to house the different views
Each tab is an independent QWidget with its own data model
Tab bar should show resource counts (e.g., 'Containers (12)', 'Images (45)')
Keep the existing ActionPanel but make it context-aware based on active tab
System tray should reflect the active tab's resource counts

phase_1_quick_wins.description: Enhance existing features with minimal new UI. Mostly backend additions to client.py and minor UI tweaks.

phase_2_core_views.description: Build the tabbed interface with new views. This is the biggest architectural change.

phase_3_advanced.description: Add detailed dialogs for inspect, pull, build operations.

phase_4_polish.description: Advanced features: terminal emulator, topology visualization, quick switcher.

technical_considerations.docker_sdk_usage: dockym already uses docker SDK (python docker package) for client connection, container listing, and stats. New features should use the same SDK for consistency.
technical_considerations.threading: All Docker API calls should run in QThreadPool via DockerPool worker pattern (already established in docker_worker.py). Never block the UI thread.
technical_considerations.error_handling: All new client.py methods should follow existing pattern: catch DockerException, APIError, NotFound and return None or empty dict.
technical_considerations.i18n: dockym uses Spanish UI labels. New features should follow the same pattern (e.g., 'Imágenes', 'Volúmenes', 'Redes').
technical_considerations.tabbed_ui: Consider using QTabWidget with custom tab bar styling to match the existing dark theme. Each tab should have its own search/filter bar.
technical_considerations.data_refresh: Each view should have its own refresh timer. Global refresh (F5) should refresh all tabs. DockerEventsWorker should notify all tabs of state changes.
